notes: LEGACY NAME as of the sensing/segmentation decoupling round: window_size now controls ONLY segment_regions'/to_awn_input's seg_len (segment length == AWN input temporal length T). It no longer controls energy_detect's smoothing window -- that is now --sensing-window-size (see next row), defaulting to window_size when unset so prior behavior is unchanged bit-for-bit (verified: SHA256-identical mask/regions/x_clean vs. the pre-decoupling code path at window_size=128, seed=0). Real 2016.10a checkpoint structurally accepts other EVEN T values (64/128/256/1024 all ran a real forward() pass without error, using dummy all-zero tensors only) because AdaptiveAvgPool1d(1) removes all T-dependence before any Linear layer -- odd T crashes (lifting-scheme even/odd split mismatch). This does NOT mean the checkpoint's classification is valid at non-128 T: it was trained specifically on 128-sample RML2016.10a segments (external/adversarial-rf/util/config.py:51, signal_len=128); no accuracy/statistical validity claim is made for any other length. behavior_test is 'partial' (structural-only, not a claim of correctness)
